title: Review & correct phase (phase 4, seeded path) — profile parser and editable section cards

impl.notes: readProfile server function reads context/profile.md at request time (not import.meta.glob). parseProfileSections splits on /^## /m boundaries; detectStatus finds first Confirmed/Derived/Unknown in body (defaults to Unknown). PhaseReviewCorrect has two render states: waiting (profile not found, AC1) and review (profile parsed, AC2). Waiting state auto-polls via checkProfileExists every 5 s and shows a Refresh button plus a 'Continue without reviewing' escape hatch. Review state renders SectionCard per section; editing updates answers.editedSections via set(), which flows through the debounced saveOnboarding to onboarding-state.json. The 'Edited' badge appears on cards where isEdited===true (AC3). Phase heading dynamically shows 'Review your profile · N sections edited' (AC3). 'Looks good' / 'Confirm all' button advances via saveNow() + next() (AC4). Fictional persona fixture labelled FICTIONAL PERSONA (AC5). hideNextButton widened to phase===4||phase===5; both paths now use internal Confirm; waiting state provides a Continue escape hatch.

userStory: As a user who bootstrapped my profile via Glean, I want to review every section the assistant wrote and correct any errors before locking it in, so that my AI context is accurate and human-verified before it seeds my OS files.

acceptanceCriteria.text: When `context/profile.md` does not yet exist, phase 4 (seeded path) shows a waiting state with a 'Refresh' button and instruction text: "After your assistant writes context/profile.md, click Refresh or wait — this page detects the file automatically."

When `context/profile.md` exists on disk, the phase parses it on `## <heading>` boundaries and renders each section as an editable card. Each card shows the section heading, body text, and a status chip. The status chip value (Confirmed, Derived, or Unknown) is parsed from the first Confirmed/Derived/Unknown label found in the section body, defaulting to Unknown if none is found.

Editing a section card's body text marks the card with a visible 'edited' badge. The phase heading shows the count of edited sections (e.g. 'Review your profile · 3 sections edited'). Edited state and body text are stored in onboarding-state.json so they survive a page refresh.

Sections that have not been edited show no badge. A 'Confirm all' or 'Looks good' action is available to accept all sections without editing and advance to the corrections handoff (story 011) or phase 5.

Any mock profile data used in development or as a Storybook/test fixture represents a FICTIONAL persona — no real names, real roles, or real org data from any worked example. A comment in the mock file explicitly labels it as fictional (e.g. `// FICTIONAL PERSONA — do not replace with real data`). `grep -rn 'FICTIONAL' work-hq/src/' returns at least one match.

technicalNotes: Add phase 4 seeded-path component (PhaseReviewCorrect) to `work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx`. Profile parser: split file content on `/^## /m` boundaries; for each section, extract heading and body; scan body for first occurrence of 'Confirmed', 'Derived', or 'Unknown' to set status chip. Store edited sections as `editedSections: Record<string, { body: string; edited: boolean }>` in onboarding-state.json answers. Read profile.md via a server function (file read at request time, not import.meta.glob, since context/profile.md is gitignored and outside docs/data/). Auto-detect poll: lightweight server function `check-profile-exists` (reuse from story 009 if already created). Phase 4 rendering branches on `answers.seedingPath` — 'bootstrap' renders this component; 'manual' renders story 003's interview forms.
outOfScope: Profile corrections handoff and lock-in (story 011), the manual-path interview forms (story 003), the scaffold-files server function (story 004), writing any OS file from this phase.

verificationPlan.checks: Returns at least one match confirming a fictional-persona label is present in the mock/fixture data.
